description:
You are the site administrator for a small web application.  
A list of user accounts (`/home/user/accounts/users.json`) and a JSON-Schema (`/home/user/accounts/users_schema.json`) are already present on the system.

Your job is to:
1. Validate that *the entire* `users.json` document conforms to `users_schema.json`.  
   • If the document is valid, you must record the success in a plain-text log file called  
     `/home/user/accounts/validation.log`.  
   • The first line of that file **must read exactly** (including spelling, spacing and the final period)  
     `OK: users.json matches users_schema.json`

2. Using `jq`, create a new text file called `/home/user/accounts/active_users.txt` that contains only the
   `username` field of every account whose `"active"` attribute is `true`, one username per line,
   sorted alphabetically (ASCII sort), with a single trailing newline at the end of the file.
   For the provided data that will be:
   alice  
   carol  
   dave  

3. Append a second line to `/home/user/accounts/validation.log` that **must read exactly**  
   `OK: active user list written to active_users.txt (3 users)`

Summary of files that must exist when you finish (exact byte-for-byte contents will be checked):

• /home/user/accounts/active_users.txt  
  alice  
  carol  
  dave  
  ⏎ (single trailing newline)

• /home/user/accounts/validation.log  
  OK: users.json matches users_schema.json  
  OK: active user list written to active_users.txt (3 users)  
  ⏎ (single trailing newline after the second line)

Do not edit either of the two source JSON files.  
No root privileges are available; stay inside the user’s home directory.  
Feel free to use any command-line tools installed in a typical minimal Linux environment (e.g. `jq`, `python3`, etc.).  
The whole job can be done with three or four commands.
name: task_000000_2e2d31bc
